I agree with the previous assessment that abdominal pain (option A) does not represent an established feature of acute rheumatic fever among those listed. The primary manifestations include carditis, polyarthritis, erythema marginatum, and chorea—none of which typically present as abdominal pain unless there's another concurrent gastrointestinal issue unrelated to rheumatic activity. Additionally, although less common than some others on the list, epistaxis could still occur due to systemic inflammation affecting blood vessels; however, its absence here makes it stand out more distinctly against typical presentations.
